mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity prices by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, so lower policy rates should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if rolling 3-year rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which should pressure corporate revenues and raise recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus three months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional hit rate is indistinguishable from unconditional returns at p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is accelerating while HOUST remains weak, the economy is showing consumer demand resilience but insufficient housing support; that combination implies earnings support without an overheating tightening response, favoring positive near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and HOUST flat-to-down does not outperform single-series effects, or if the interaction term has no positive incremental explanatory power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized downturn in INDPRO and PAYEMS signals broad-based production and payroll contraction, which should directly weaken expected corporate earnings and push S&P 500 returns negative over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in production and payrolls fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or if the combined signal is weaker than either proxy alone across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST above its recent trend reflects housing demand stabilization and future construction activity, which should lead broad equity market gains as cyclical growth expectations improve over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 5% from its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts are followed by negative or zero-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal’s hit rate is not materially above 50% after trigger dates.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is low and FEDFUNDS is near the lower bound, real-rate pressure is contained and liquidity conditions are supportive, so equities should post positive 5-day returns as rate volatility falls.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.50% and CPIAUCSL monthly inflation below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-inflation/low-rate regime does not produce higher 5-day SPX returns than non-trigger periods, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive and stable across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently weak PAYEMS reading combined with high UNRATE indicates labor-market slack that reduces consumption growth and earnings momentum, so the equity market should underperform over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if weak payrolls plus high unemployment do not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal loses significance after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 relative to prior quarters signals broad final-demand contraction that should eventually compress earnings expectations and lead negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal’s out-of-sample rank IC remains below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.
